dry_bulk.confluence_note: Spot rates and the BDI are at the 99th percentile with strong positive momentum, yet Breakwave reports maintain a 'negative' fundamental outlook due to China's steel sector weakness.
dry_bulk.summary: Despite the BDI entering a Z=+2.22 expansion regime, the disconnect between record spot rates and underlying demand remains stark. Chinese iron ore inventories are near all-time highs (166mt, +20.3% YOY) while steel production continues to contract (-3.6% YOY).
dry_bulk.outlook: Expect spot rate volatility to increase as high prices face resistance from deteriorating industrial fundamentals in China.
dry_bulk.watch: Iron ore inventory drawdown rates at Chinese ports and potential stimulus impacts on steel production.

tanker.confluence_note: Extreme quantitative expansion (Clean Z=+3.14) is strongly supported by positive fundamental sentiment driven by geopolitical disruptions and unnavigable straits.
tanker.summary: The tanker market is reacting to a structural shift as the 'worst-case' geopolitical scenario has materialized, forcing a re-routing of global oil flows. Clean tanker rates have exploded (ROC60 +147.3%), while dirty tanker fundamentals remain robust despite a slight cooling in recent momentum.
tanker.outlook: Bullish conditions are expected to persist as long as geopolitical constraints limit transit through primary maritime chokepoints.
tanker.watch: Middle East conflict escalation and global crude-on-water inventory levels which have trended lower.

macro_note: Global shipping is currently defined by a high-price environment where tanker strength is driven by supply-side shocks and dry bulk strength is sustained by momentum despite softening industrial demand signals.
